codeReview.summary: The feature correctly adds one shared recursive normalization seam for nested foreign payloads and covers the required raw-query string record-link case without regressing existing nested object-form hydration. The implementation stays scoped to read/decode behavior and preserves reference-only storage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker had to discover by experimentation that the required acceptance command initially matched zero tests because the exact regression name was missing. This mission-specific expectation for exact hydrate-roundtrip regression names is not captured in shared library guidance, so workers may waste time rediscovering it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker 544dee43-22c2-4960-b977-955d3d263437 shows `cargo test -p appdb --test integration_db raw_query_string_record_links_decode_into_foreign_models -- --exact --nocapture` first returning 0 tests, after which the worker added the named regression.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill says to inspect the real RecordId serde contract before manual normalization, but it does not explicitly call out that hydrate-roundtrip workers should also verify exact acceptance-test names exist before assuming a runtime failure. This gap contributed to the worker needing to treat test absence as the initial failure mode.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill step 20 discusses inspecting RecordId serde shape, while the transcript shows the first acceptance command for this feature failed with 0 tests and required adding the exact named regression before runtime validation could proceed.

summary: Pass. Reviewed the feature transcript skeleton, handoff, commit fde1746c324bb21c3b1244bb85aa7af820e71342 diff, skill guidance, and mission shared-state files. The change centralizes recursive foreign-link normalization through the repository read/decode seam and adds the exact raw-query regression required by VAL-HYDRATE-003; I found no blocking or non-blocking code issues within this feature scope.
